<tool_call>
Consider two qubits in the pure state  
\(|\psi\rangle = \sqrt{0.3}\,|00\rangle + \sqrt{0.7}\,|11\rangle\).  
Compute the von Neumann entanglement entropy of the reduced density matrix of the first qubit.
<tool_call>
-> State: |\psi\rangle = \sqrt{0.3}\,|00\rangle + \sqrt{0.7}\,|11\rangle
<tool_call>
-> Reduced density matrix \rho_1 = \operatorname{Tr}_2(|\psi\rangle\langle\psi|) = \begin{pmatrix}0.3 & 0 \\ 0 & 0.7\end{pmatrix}
<tool_call>
-> Eigenvalues of \rho_1: \lambda_1 = 0.3,\ \lambda_2 = 0.7
<tool_call>
-> Von Neumann entropy S = -\sum_i \lambda_i \log_2 \lambda_i = 0.881\ \text{bits}
<tool_call>
-> \boxed{0.881\ \text{bits}}

Water flows through a smooth circular pipe of diameter 0.05 m at a velocity of 2 m/s. The water density is 998 kg/m³ and the dynamic viscosity is 0.001 Pa·s. Compute the Reynolds number, determine the flow regime, calculate the Darcy friction factor using the Blasius correlation, and find the pressure drop per metre of pipe.
-> Reynolds number: 9.98e4
-> Flow regime: turbulent (Re > 4000)
-> Darcy friction factor (Blasius): 0.0178
-> Pressure drop per metre: 7.10e2 Pa/m
-> \boxed{Re\approx9.98\times10^4,\; f\approx0.0178,\; \Delta P/L\approx7.10\times10^2\ \text{Pa/m}}

A first‑order irreversible reaction A → B occurs in a constant‑volume batch reactor. The rate constant follows the Arrhenius expression k = A·exp(−Ea/(RT)). Given pre‑exponential factor A = 2.5×10⁷ s⁻¹, activation energy Ea = 85 kJ·mol⁻¹, initial concentration CA0 = 2.0 mol·L⁻¹, and temperature T = 500 K, calculate the time required to achieve 90 % conversion of A.
ba
Pre‑exponential factor A = 2.5e7 s⁻¹
Activation energy Ea = 85000 J·mol⁻¹
Temperature T = 500 K
Gas constant R = 8.314 J·mol⁻¹·K⁻¹
ba
Rate constant k = A * exp(−Ea/(R·T))
k = 0.0325 s⁻¹
ba
Desired conversion X = 0.90
For first‑order kinetics: t = −ln(1−X)/k
ba
t = −ln(0.10)/0.0325 s⁻¹
t = 70.9 s
ba
\boxed{70.9\text{ s}}
